summary_text: 1. **Must-simulate cases**: positive path where `implement.result=success`, `codex_exit=0`, `kernel_run_id` present and finalize step runs with real args; skip path where any one gate fails (`codex_exit!=0` or empty `kernel_run_id`) and no backup record is created; both runner modes (`EXTERNAL_BACKUP_TOOL_AVAILABLE=true/false`) plus lock-contention path (`task-completion-backup.lock` exists) to confirm explicit record behavior and exit codes; dispatch behavior with `NO_GHA=false` (attempted dispatch) and `NO_GHA=true` (no dispatch) using the exact same `--assistant/--source/--session-id/--summary` shape as workflow line 834+. 2. **Acceptable shortcuts**: run `scripts/local/run-kernel-task-completion-backup.sh` directly in an isolated temp sandbox (`STATE_ROOT`, `CODEX_HOME`, `TOOLS_ROOT`) instead of whole workflow; stub `gh` in `PATH` to capture/verify dispatch payloads without network side effects; use synthetic run IDs and local temp journal/sqlite assertions as pass criteria instead of real GitHub issue/PR interactions from the `Update issue with results` step. 3. **Failure modes to watch**: accidental live dispatch to default `GHA_REPO=cursorvers/fugue-orchestrator` when `NO_GHA` is not forced in simulation; silent success with no record when lock is busy (script exits `0` after “explicit record skipped”); environment drift between external-tool and fallback paths causing different artifacts; direct-call integration missing `kernel-run-complete.sh` side effects (phase-gate/compact update/workspace receipt), which can pass backup simulation but still leave kernel lifecycle metadata incomplete.
